references.excerpt_text: Diuretic and anti-inflammatory decoction for Shotha (edema), Jvara, Kasa, anemia, and ascites.

explanation.summary: Punarnavadi Kashayam is the preferred choice when generalized inflammatory swelling (Shotha) is present alongside fever and cough. Punarnava's diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties directly address the systemic edematous component. This presentation does NOT match Vyaghryadi's specialization in upper respiratory URTI/Pinasa.

claims.text: Shotha (edema/systemic inflammation) is a primary indication of Punarnavadi Kashayam.
Punarnava (Boerhavia diffusa) has Shothaghna (anti-edematous) and diuretic properties that address the systemic inflammatory component.
Vyaghryadi Kashayam, while sharing Jvara and Kasa indication, lacks specific Shotha (edema) targeting.

results.differentiation_note: Best suited when generalized inflammatory swelling (Shotha) is the dominant feature — differs from Vyaghryadi which targets upper respiratory URTI.

references.excerpt_text: Indicated in Jvara and Kasa — specializes in Pinasa/URTI. Does not address Shotha.

explanation.summary: Vyaghryadi matches Jvara and Kasa but does NOT have Shotha (edema) as an indication. For systemic inflammatory presentations, Punarnavadi is the superior choice.

results.differentiation_note: More suitable for Pinasa (URTI/common cold) than for systemic Shotha.

disclaimer: Educational decision support only. Not a diagnosis or prescription. Clinical judgment of a qualified vaidya is required.
